paper_focus.title: Overview of Lightweight Ferrous Materials: Strategies and Promises
paper_focus.research_objective: To provide a comprehensive overview of design strategies, metallurgy, properties, and processing challenges for low-density steels, with emphasis on bulk iron-based alloys, in order to evaluate their potential for automotive weight savings.
material_systems: Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels (ferritic, austenitic, duplex, triplex phase)
Steel-matrix composites (reinforced with TiC, VC, SiC, TiB2, VB2, Cr2B3, Si3N4, Al2O3)
Steel-based laminates (steel-Al alloy, steel-glass fiber/SiC/graphite layers)
main_process_variables: Synthesis/fabrication routes (liquid metal casting, powder compaction and sintering, self-propagating high-temperature synthesis, injection molding, cladding, roll bonding)
Foaming methods (liquid metallurgy, powder metallurgy, bubbling agents)
Heat treatments (annealing, overaging of duplex alloys)
expected_information_axis: mechanical_properties (strength, ductility, Young's modulus, specific stiffness)
strengthening_mechanisms (precipitation hardening, solid solution strengthening, TRIP, TWIP, SIP, MBIP)
